node.text:
This is the definition of your VPN vs the one she tells you not to worry about. I combined two amazing technologies into one symbiotic application that leverages their strengths together to help others and their families stay protected against unwarranted surveillance and censorship in some countries. 

🔗: https://github.com/0xXyc/SwizGuard
•
•
•
•
•
#hacking #surveillance #vpn #swizsecurity #privacy

"you don't trust consumer VPNs, you want actual privacy without the BS" 🤓☝️ *uses a VPS* it's cool and has its own use case, but that isn't "actual privacy" unless it's enterprise level security, and even that is based on a promise of someone. You don't actually own the keys to anything except the PC sitting in your house, and the CPU is backdoored 😭😭😭
@frio.ye dying to know what your setup is

@jakeswiz They have a built-in backdoor on my CPU, alike yours. They won, there doesn't need to be any setup. Don't be wetarded and use public wifi/weak passwords/etc, but nothing special, just raw dogging it with acceptance that privacy is imaginary at this point. Who is your VPS provider? (Tbh I harden my router and turn off risky services that are unused, and monitor network access/outlier IP destinations, and a good ole VPN bc fuck my ISP, feddy fedz bear know what you're doing at all times tho. Zero day everything.
Wow.... This guy sure is a pro at toggling shadowsocks... Real 1337 shit here...
@moneyoffthefloor completely different but whatever you wanna think

@jakeswiz I just watched the start of this reel initially and scrolled past before actually looking at your work. Now that I've looked, it is exactly what I expected. You just made a setup script for XTLS and wrote a first person narrative in your readme. This is literally just a setup script for the modern equivalent of shadowsocks nigga. I understand this isn't worthless for non technical users, but you didn't do anything here besides make a bash script for v2ray 😭
Ok but... what's the point of wrapping a vpn tunnel inside another tls packet?
Nice sing-box wrapper. I built somthing similar but I like how you streamlined the setup process.
Using mullvad is more safer than others vpns?
@frio.ye dying to know what your setup is

@jakeswiz They have a built-in backdoor on my CPU, alike yours. They won, there doesn't need to be any setup. Don't be wetarded and use public wifi/weak passwords/etc, but nothing special, just raw dogging it with acceptance that privacy is imaginary at this point. Who is your VPS provider? (Tbh I harden my router and turn off risky services that are unused, and monitor network access/outlier IP destinations, and a good ole VPN bc fuck my ISP, feddy fedz bear know what you're doing at all times tho. Zero day everything.

node.text: @realsyedmuzammil leta debate on this if the Mullahs destroyed the Iranian Civilisation
Tu khud expose ho chuka hai🤣🤣
I think word Mullah is not good

Bhai Muzamil Ko koi serious Nahi leta muu terha karke ya english 2-3 lafz maarke koi journalist Nahi bnta ap Ko non biased reviews dene hote Hain And 2nd thing ap Muslim ho West Ki journalism and Yahan Ki journalism mein farq Hona chaiye Woh religion Ko Nahi dekhte lekin Humein dekhna Hai Tou Yeh liberal nihayat biased shaks lehaza Usko ignore karein
Yeh to ha hi ch0tea sala landy ka philosopher 🍌😂🤡 ha yeh muzamil barha 😂😂😂
Tu khud expose ho chuka hai🤣🤣

node.text:
I didn't see this coming....Milla Jovovich made a memory tool for AI.....yes...

THAT Milla Jovovich (The Fifth Element, Resident Evil, Zoolander)

And it's actually pretty good; it's the highest scoring AI memory tool....and it's free.

From supreme being, zombie-slayer, and unhinged runway assassin… to open‑source developer. 2026 is wild.
Since I found out that you can buy GitHub stars ...

memory is the real unlock — stateless agents burn half their compute rebuilding context every call. if MemPalace actually scores that high on the benchmarks, persistent memory stops being a side-project and becomes a primitive. open-source version matters more than the actor angle tbh.
It’s a pure bullshit !!! I tested it it burns more tokens than Claude itself
How much did they pay you? Cut cam coffee cut cam, tra tra! 😂
Can we use memplace and Graphify inside obsidian together? Power users help 🤓🤓🤓🤓
I did try it last week and it has potential a bit buggy! But work and very light

memory is the real unlock — stateless agents burn half their compute rebuilding context every call. if MemPalace actually scores that high on the benchmarks, persistent memory stops being a side-project and becomes a primitive. open-source version matters more than the actor angle tbh.
Since I found out that you can buy GitHub stars ...

postRawGql.accessibility_caption: Photo shared by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026 tagging @coder.shubh, @rootxploit.0x, @ha4kerslegacy, @cybermastery, and @h4cker_sakib. May be a graphic of poster, magazine and text that says '1234 E 中1 FAKE 방는 3282 07/24 CHNENO HOW TO KEEP COVERTIDENTITY COVERT IDENTITY Spy & Hacker Perspective ZeroDay Security'.

node.accessibility_caption: Photo shared by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026 tagging @coder.shubh, @rootxploit.0x, @ha4kerslegacy, @cybermastery, and @h4cker_sakib. May be a graphic of poster, magazine and text that says '1234 E 中1 FAKE 방는 3282 07/24 CHNENO HOW TO KEEP COVERTIDENTITY COVERT IDENTITY Spy & Hacker Perspective ZeroDay Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster and text that says 'IDENTITY CONTROL OFFENSIVE MINDSET Uses •Keeps identities separate across platforms •Avoids obvious personal details different personas for different contexts •Tries to maintain a consistent backstory Limits direct interaction with real-life contacts REALITY •Writing style, language, and tone reveal patterns •Small overlaps (timing, interests) connect identities •Reused habits across platforms expose links •People forget details under pressure •OSINT tools correlate fragmented data ZeroDaySecurity ZeroDay Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of magazine, poster and text that says 'COVER COVER&ROUTE & ROUTE CONTROL OFFENSIVE MINDSET •Maintains a normal-looking looking lifestyle ·Uses believable daily routines Avoids unusual behavior Blends into common roles Keeps movements "natural" REALITY •Repeated timing patterns become visible Unusual consistency can look suspicious •Routine = predictable footprint •Investigators map movement timelines Deviations under stress expose identity ZeroDaySecurity ZeroDay Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster, magazine and text that says 'DIGITAL PRESENCE CONTROL OFFENSIVE MINDSET Keeps activity minimal •Avoids unnecessary interactions •Limits posting and engagement •Uses different platforms separately Tries to stay "low profile" REALITY •Even minimal activity creates logs •Login times form patterns •Metadata exists everywhere •Cross-platform behavior gets linked •Silence itself can become a pattern ZeroDay Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster and text that says 'ENVIRONMENT AWARENESS OFFENSIVE MINDSET •Keeps work and personal environments separate •Avoids mixing contexts •Uses controlled setups •Maintains minimal digital footprint •Limits ・ exposure of tools REALITY •Devices still create unique signatures •Environment patterns get noticed •Reuse of habits exposes linkage •Small configuration overlaps reveal identity •Forensics connects environments over time ZeroDay ZeroDaySecurity Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster and text that says 'PHYSICAL AWARENESS OFFENSIVE MINDSET •Blends into crowds •Avoids attention •Moves naturally in public spaces •Stays aware of surroundings Keeps interactions minimal REALITY •Movement patterns can be tracked •Repeated locations form profiles •Timing + location strong identifier •Public environments are heavily observed •Witnesses data fill gaps ZeroDay ZeroDaySecurity Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster and text that says 'CORRELATION SETUP OFFENSIVE MINDSET Keeps everything separate •Avoids obvious links •Believes "no direct connection = safe" •Focuses on hiding individual actions REALITY •Small data points get connected •Time, behavior, and context align ·Indirect links reveal identity •OSINT builds full profiles from •Correlation defeats isolation tragments ZeroDay Security'.

Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be a graphic of poster and text that says 'FINALTRUTH FINAL TRUTH Human error Humanerror>technicalfailure > technical failure ZeroDay ZeroDaySecurity Security'.
Photo by Praveen | Cyber Intelligence on April 04, 2026. May be an image of text that says 'Follow for more more THANK YOU ZeroDay Security'.
